speaker: MODERATOR
text: Senator, the first question goes to you. Thirty seconds.

speaker: HOOL
text: Thank you. Let's talk about the record, because the record matters.

speaker: HOOL
text: I was the only one on this stage who opposed that bailout from day one.

speaker: HOOL
text: Not after the polls moved — from day one.

speaker: HOOL
text: My opponent voted for it twice, then pretended he didn't.

speaker: MODERATOR
text: Would you sign the relief bill as written? Yes or no.

speaker: HOOL
text: I'll give you a straight answer: yes, on day one, I would sign it.

speaker: RIVAL
text: That's easy to say when you've never had to govern.

speaker: MODERATOR
text: Senator — same question to you. Would you sign it?

speaker: RIVAL
text: Well, that's a complicated question and it depends on many factors.

speaker: MODERATOR
text: Is that a yes or a no?

speaker: RIVAL
text: I think we need to study this carefully before committing.

speaker: HOOL
text: And there it is — the same dodge we've heard for years.

speaker: HOOL
text: Let me be clear about exactly where I stand on this.

speaker: HOOL
text: Working families don't get to say 'it's complicated' when rent is due.

speaker: HOOL
text: You've had twelve years to fix this and you did nothing.

speaker: MODERATOR
text: [crowd applause] — order, please, let's have order.

speaker: HOOL
text: I'm not here to make promises, I'm here to make decisions.

speaker: HOOL
text: And on day one, those decisions start with the people in this room.

speaker: MODERATOR
text: We'll take a short break and return with closing statements.
